hypothesis: Cross-cluster gradient routing through capability-aware task decomposition — instead of routing whole tasks to single agents, decompose tasks into sub-components matched to each agent's strongest gradient cluster, then reassemble results. This creates cross-cluster gradient amplification without redundant overlap.
research_question: Can task decomposition into agent-specific sub-components produce higher evolution signal than whole-task routing to a single agent?
expected_outcome: Establish a capability-aware decomposition protocol that maximizes cross-cluster gradient amplification while minimizing redundant agent cycles
